activeProjects.text: Debug OpenClaw Anthropic auth/model selection in Telegram DM

whereWeLeftOff.text: sarahmat0816 gog re-auth complete with password 'clawd'; GOG_KEYRING_PASSWORD=clawd set globally in openclaw.json; both thematweiss and sarahmat0816 Gmail/Calendar working; Hermes TOOLS.md updated
Confirmed Anthropic failure is not from openclaw.json, auth.json, shell env, systemd service env, deleted auth-profiles.json, or /root/.claude/.credentials.json.
Confirmed openclaw-gateway runs as systemd user service openclaw-gateway.service with no ANTHROPIC/CLAUDE env vars in the daemon process.
Found root cause in /root/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json: active Telegram DM session agent:main:telegram:direct:8001393940 contains authProfileOverride = anthropic:oauth-sub.
This stale session-scoped auth override is likely rehydrating the malformed Anthropic OAuth token and causing Headers.append invalid header value errors.
Next step: inspect surrounding lines in sessions.json, then either remove authProfileOverride from the affected session entry or reset/delete that session so OpenClaw recreates it cleanly.

sopTruths.text: When auth cleanup fails, inspect session-scoped state under /root/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json for authProfileOverride or model/provider pinning.
For OpenClaw user services, inspect systemd with systemctl --user cat <unit> --no-pager and live daemon env via /proc/<pid>/environ rather than relying on shell env.
Do not paste live secrets/tokens into chat; rotate any exposed gateway, bot, or API tokens after debugging.
Bachelor party return flight note: J Lee is also on the shared return flight (United 2679, EWR → LAX, Mon Apr 6, 10:10 AM ET).

notes.text: User asked to log this to memory, especially if there is a solution.
